name: Heart of the Elders
intro_text: Deep in the jungle you find what the Eztli called the Mouth of K'n-yan — a vast cave opening descending into the earth. The air rising from below smells of stone and something older than time.
This scenario is played in two parts. In Part 1, you must find the pattern that opens the Mouth. In Part 2, you descend into the subterranean realm of K'n-yan itself.
The creatures below are not of this age. The deeper you go, the less the normal rules seem to apply.
setup_instructions: Part 1: Place the Mouth of K'n-yan (start revealed) plus Time-Wracked Woods and Stone Altar face-down.
Investigators begin at the Mouth of K'n-yan. Discover clues to advance Act 1 and open the entrance.
Part 2 begins when Act 2 advances. Place all K'n-yan underground locations. The aboveground locations are no longer accessible.
The subterranean locations have their own rules: torches provide shroud reduction, and the darkness trait applies special penalties.
Investigators must reach Descent to Yoth to complete the scenario.

resolutions.R1: You descend to Yoth and find what lies beneath. Record in the campaign log: *The investigators descended into K'n-yan.* Each investigator earns 3 experience.
resolutions.R2: You escape K'n-yan but cannot reach Yoth. Record: *The investigators escaped K'n-yan without descending further.* Each investigator earns 1 experience.
resolutions.defeat: K'n-yan closes around you. Each investigator suffers 2 physical trauma.
